children.heading: Allow
children.label: 1 Allow
children.text: The term “allow”, with respect to the diversion through a country of goods, services, or technologies, means the government of the country knows or has reason to know that the territory of the country is being used for such diversion.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs, the Committee on Foreign Relations, and the Select Committee on Intelligence of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Affairs and the Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Commerce Control List
children.label: 3 Commerce Control List
children.text: The term “Commerce Control List” means the list maintained pursuant to part 774 of the Export Administration Regulations (or any corresponding similar regulation or ruling).

children.heading: Divert; diversion
children.label: 4 Divert; diversion
children.text: The terms “divert” and “diversion” refer to the transfer or release, directly or indirectly, of a good, service, or technology to an end-user or an intermediary that is not an authorized recipient of the good, service, or technology.

children.heading: End-user
children.label: 5 End-user
children.text: The term “end-user”, with respect to a good, service, or technology, means the person that receives and ultimately uses the good, service, or technology.

children.heading: Export Administration Regulations
children.label: 6 Export Administration Regulations
children.text: The term “Export Administration Regulations” means subchapter C of chapter VII of title 15, Code of Federal Regulations (or any corresponding similar regulation or ruling).

children.heading: Government
children.label: 7 Government
children.text: The term “government” includes any agency or instrumentality of a government.

children.heading: Intermediary
children.label: 8 Intermediary
children.text: The term “intermediary” means a person that receives a good, service, or technology while the good, service, or technology is in transit to the end-user of the good, service, or technology.

children.heading: International Traffic in Arms Regulations
children.label: 9 International Traffic in Arms Regulations
children.text: The term “International Traffic in Arms Regulations” means subchapter M of chapter I of title 22, Code of Federal Regulations (or any corresponding similar regulation or ruling).

children.heading: Iran
children.label: 10 Iran
children.text: The term “Iran” includes the Government of Iran and any agency or instrumentality of Iran.

children.heading: Iranian end-user
children.label: 11 Iranian end-user
children.text: The term “Iranian end-user” means an end-user that is the Government of Iran or a person in, or an agency or instrumentality of, Iran.

children.heading: Iranian intermediary
children.label: 12 Iranian intermediary
children.text: The term “Iranian intermediary” means an intermediary that is the Government of Iran or a person in, or an agency or instrumentality of, Iran.

children.label: A
children.text: section 4605(j)(1)(A) of title 50 (or any successor thereto);1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: United States Munitions List
children.label: 14 United States Munitions List
children.text: The term “United States Munitions List” means the list maintained pursuant to part 121 of the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (or any corresponding similar regulation or ruling).
